reviews.review_msg: I don't recommend taking a class with him. He is very disorganized and just reads definitions off the slides. Does not explain the material very well.

terrible, avoid at all costs. lazy and unapproachable. waste of time and money. he did not know material, read definitions off slides that were already from textbook. cannot teach what so ever. ta's taught us 10x more than he did. take it with someone else
He was a terrible instructor, lectures were uninformative and pointless and he was not very well organized

Worst prof ever! He never seems to know what he's doing. Lectures were a waste of time. Always end lecture in an hour when we paid for 2 every week. The TAs were way more knowledgeable than him. Avoid him at all cost.
Decent Prof, no complaints. I would take the class with him again. lectures weren't boring.
Jessie is an awful prof. He was never there for his office hours and when he was to be found his answer would be to tell me to go to the T.A's office hours on a different day.
Jessie was an awful prof. Lectures were useless and he was very slow at providing course materials. Take 207 with someone else.

Great prof that keeps lectures entertaining and talks about real-world problems. The exams are straight forward and similar to practice and his finance training package is a must-buy, some multiple choice from there are copied and pasted in exams. Be aware of the TA's, they are tough graders and having the right answer won't guarantee full marks.
great class, he came across quite rude and egoistic in the beginning, but ended the semester strong
Great Prof he kept everyone very engaged and was very clear in grading, group projects were kind of a bit of work but it was very similar to questions on the final so great practice.

Emrul was honestly an amazing prof. I was a bit worried at the beginning of the semester, but he provided a ton of resources to help you succeed in what is a pretty difficult course. His tests were very similar to the finance training practice, so as long as you put in the reps, you'll be set. He really sets everyone up for success. TAKE HIS CLASS!
One of the best profs i've had at sfu, super kind and engaging! he uses real life examples which actually help you learn. definitely take a class with him if you can.

Professor Emrul is kind and helpful. The exams were fair since he only tested you on the the materials that were taught in the lecture. He also offered some bonus mark to boost your score. In addition, he will give you a lot of practice questions to prepare you for the midterm or finals.

Emrul was a great prof you can tell he wants the best for his students. His exams are really fair and like practice questions. I recommend getting finance training over thetextbook. Finance training has a lot of practice and explains concepts simply and MC from exams is similar to the ones from FT. Great prof overall
Amazing professor, would love to take more courses with Emrul. He makes classes very engaging and personal.

One of if not the best professors and learning experiences I have had in my education. Makes complicated concepts easy to understand, exams have no surprises and the teaching team prepares the class for the midterm and final exams. Take his class if its avalible you truly won't have a better learning experience in your time at uni
One of the best profs that actually cares that you pass the course and learn the material. This is coming from someone who never writes rate my profs
One of best professors I have ever had
You genuinely may never have the opportunity to take a class with a prof as great as Emrul in your life. Don't miss out.

Definitely recommend taking bus 207 with him. He makes a class everyone says is difficult, feel easy. Super clear expectations for exams and emphasizes what we need to know during class. Overall very nice professor who makes going to class enjoyable.

Emrul is an outstanding professor who makes learning difficult content so much easier through carefully explaining and applying the concepts described. There really is nobody better to take BUS 207 with.

Highly recommend Dr. Hasan. Come to lectures, as they are engaging and he gives real-world insights for everything. Dr. Hasan wants you to pass and he wants you to succeed. If he is teaching a class, take it.

Professor Emrul is an outstanding instructor for 207. His lectures were engaging, connecting real-world concepts to bring managerial economics to life. The course is challenging and requires a lot of effort to get a good mark, but Professor Emrul's teaching style makes the material much more approachable and fun.

Amazing lecturer. 3 hour lectures passed by like nothing, very engaging and funny but also very passionate about what he teaches. Provides real world examples which help relate to the course content. Favourite professor I've had so far in my undergrad. Emrul is a gem.

Professor Hasan brings the course material to life with engaging real-world examples. Though the workload is demanding, his teaching style and commitment to student success make BUS207 an enriching experience. unstuckstudy ai was a helpful study tool alongside the course resources.

I don't normally give a 5/5 ratings but Emrul definitely deserves it. He makes class fun and explains the concepts very well. The course itself is hard but he made the lectures entertaining so the 3 hour classes didn't feel that long. However you need to put in the work for a good mark. I totally recommend taking 207 with him!

I didn't like the guy at the start. He skipped two weeks of our schedule due to absences, but as the weeks went on, his philosophy of respect, and justice where due reflected. All exams and projects were fair, and lectures were engaging despite the difficulty of its content. I kind of owe him one because he made economics fun again.  Thanks Emrul.

Prof. Hasan genuinely cares about the success of his students. He knows the material very well so he doesn't usually teach from slides but rather takes a more organic approach to teaching. He is very good at simplifying tough concepts by drawing his own graphs and diagrams. Easily one of my favorite profs at SFU. A very nice person in general.

Heads up, there are 80 question MyLabs for each chapter, prof is a lil disorganized, and crazy group assignments. All in all though, prof was funny. I failed bus 207 before with Karen Ruckman and this man pulled me through the mud. Thanks Emrul, missing you everyday

The course is hard but it was one of the relevant courses I was able to apply to my life. His real-life examples, stories, and teachings made the class very fun and engaging. At times he's a bit unorganized but it wasn't a big deal as you just have to review the assignments, mylab and textbook to succeed in the course.
He was a good prof. Funny. Engaging. Man knows it all.

Great professor so far. Really knowledgeable on the subject and manages to explain the ideas in an easy-to-understand manner. The course isn't too difficult if you follow along with the lectures since the prof provides several examples and practice questions which are often harder than the examples seen on the midterms.
He's definitely a professor who teaches. But super handsome guy, definitely a dilf makes class interesting .
Great prof! Don't know why there's so many bad reviews..Her notes explain everything and she clarifies any questions at the end..couldn't ask for any better

I had him as a TA. I attended the first two classes before I cstopped. When you ask him a question, he gives you a blank stare that says "how can you not know this". If I knew, why am I taking this course? I'd spend 2 hours in other TA's office instead of going to one hour of his tutorial and feeling like a dumbass. He's RUDE and ARROGANT.
Speaks a bit too fast
No lecture note other than the one by his hand writing, and his hand writing is terrible. Explanation is really hard to understand.
Horrible prof, tanked my gpa b/c of this class. marking is unreasonable and notes are useless. basically gotta teach yourself the course
Dont take it with matadeen!!!!!!! Material was super dry plus exams were incredibly hard and unbelievable. Everyone did so bad and it was a gpa killer. AVOID
Omg dont take bus 207 with this prof! Cant explain anything and is a gpa killer. U WILL FAIL like only a few ppl manage to do okay in this course. Dont take it with this prof
Omg guys dont take bus 207 with him!! His finals r so bad oh god! take it with someone else for sure. You will fail his class, he doesnt scale, so hard exams, ITS A DISASTER
GPA KILLER! final exam is very tough and assignments are not related to the textbook.   theory questions and T or F are quite difficult
Easy course, if you are good at math 0.0  just take the notes, do the assignment questions, and think for a little bit. Believe it or not, I got full mark on his final.

His classes are fun, he jokes all the time and makes it easier to undertsand. However, I recommned to take this class with a different professor, he is not helpful at all and his final is so hard, it is not related to any of the assignments you do, it will bring your GPA down!
Dont take his class! Absolute not recommended.. take it with a different professor if possible, gpa killer
She acts as if she's doing you a favor by teaching the course. Bad attitude.

This professor does not go over any practice questions relevant to the homework, leading you to seek resources outside of class to complete assignments. The slides don't tend to be very relevant either, and the professor does a lot of talking but not explaining.

4th year student retaking 207 with Mohamad here. Ignore the bad reviews from salty 2nd years. Mohamad explains concepts a LOT better than other profs - he tells you what you need to know & why. First midterm was very similar to the practice Q's. Gives out bonus marks like candy and makes this difficult class somewhat easy. Nice guy. Take him.

Lectures and explanations are very unclear and the slides are not helpful at all. He doesn't do a good job of explaining even simple concepts. He also comes off like he doesn't care about the students and is too good to give effort to answer your questions. Grading is based 100% on exams. Be prepared to learn everything yourself if you take him.

His exams have like 10 questions lol if you get one multiple choice wrong thats like -10 marks. You also have to explain your multiple choice answer thats worth half the marks. Lectures are useless he just makes dad jokes about how his wife hates him. Only time i enjoyed the lecture is one time when he made fun of himself for being bald.

Be ready to teach everything yourself because the lectures and lecture slides are not helpful for preparing for exams. The grading was based 100% on exams but he also assigns weekly assignments. If you do them, you get nothing. If you don't do them, you lose 3% of your entire grade for each missing assignment with no late submissions. Bad prof.
Awful class! The professor is always late, only uses 2 hours of lecture in a 3-hour class, and cant even teach. Be prepared to learn everything on your own, this is a very hard class!

Lectures and explanations are very unclear and the slides are not helpful at all. He doesn't do a good job of explaining even simple concepts. Be prepared to learn everything yourself if you take him.

I think the reason of a lot of people wanna give him a bad rating is because the material in course is already hard enough, which cant be avoided no matter who is teaching you. I do think Allan is good, no textbook required, just come to his class and make notes of what he said and focus on weekly assignments which are similar to exams

Do not take this course with this professor. The material is already difficult and his teaching style makes the course exponentially more difficult. The first half of the semester was a lot harder than the second half. Midterm and final exam questions were nothing like the assignment questions. Weekly assignments require a lot of effort.
Probably the worst prof I've had at SFU. Avoid at all costs. Tests are nothing like assignments, lectures are impossible to follow because he talks so slow.

Allan is a good guy and is funny. Pretty mediocre teacher, he gets the material across okay but nothing special. His TAs were quite annoying though, never replying to emails and creating confusion right before exams which did not help the stress level. Assignments and exam questions were also marked extremely hard.

Not sure how he does in-person but 100% would not recommend taking his online class. He obviously has a hard time adapting to the new technology and still using the same evaluation/grading/teaching method even when EVERYTHING is different. Probably the most unorganized and irresponsible exam experience I ever had...
absolute snail at giving lectures. A snooze fest, on god. No feed back on the assignments. Please, don't take this mans class.
He is very slow during lectures. His assignments are very lengthy and very different than what he teaches in class.
If you have another choice, dont choose him.
BUS 207 is tough course but Uzair's lecture and humor made it easy for me to understand.
Kind prof. His lectures were recorded which made it easier for me to score.
I failed his class but got A from  ECON103&105 with Martin. He is not teaching ECON, he is playing and randomly teach whatever he want…
Very tough grader and made all the class content more difficult and can't be accepted!
This man is too young. He tested and given the questions all from ChatGpt. He even don't know the answer, is that how qualified to be a Beedie Prof???
Everything is messed up and not make sense at all. He made me afraid BBA during the first year..
This guy just a PHD Student, don't know what to teach. Choose another prof instead…
He is a knowledgeable and caring  professor! Love his class!

BUS708. Extensive amount of mistakes. Lectures slides aren't organized well, with just mixed information point after point. Often wasn't able to understand student's questions. Uses exentsive AI. Disorganized, often not remembering various components of his own content. He's a smart well-meaning guy, but needs to refine his teaching methods.

Funny prof, lectures were engaging but not required, everything was recorded including tutorials. TAs didnt really explain what they were doing when answering practice problems, but it was clear enough.
Nice Prof. I liked the fact that practice problems and tutorials made attempting mid terms easy.
He is a really good prof given the fact that it is a 3 hr class and he keeps us engaged.
He is very friendly and always encourages questions.
Lots of content but exams are a summary of practice questions and basic econ.

He was fine, considering that he's new. Exams weren't too stressful as he said what would mostly be on them beforehand. Sometimes his hw solutions were done by AI and had incorrect answers or methods that were not taught in class.

The teaching is all jumbled up and there is no clear structure. Beedie actually assigned a PhD student to teach BUS207. A lot of the content was created by AI. Sometimes he had no idea about the students' questions. It's truly terrifying...

As a new professor honestly not bad. Funny and engaging prof. But the slides had a lot of incorrect information and the practice sheets were written by AI a lot which made it kind of confusing. But if you go to lectures and pay attention you should do fine.

Yall, he ain't bad AT ALL. I admit, the TAs actually suck, they explained nothing and just said what they wrote down. But Uzair is decent espesh considering the fact that this is his first time teaching. He's better than some of the experienced profs that I have had. So yeah, not bad all, don't let these negative reviews scare u. He's good dw.

Uzair doesn't deserve the hate he gets. I understand this is his first time teaching a full course but he is honestly better than some of the experienced professors i've had. His course is based fully on exams 25% midtermx2 and 50% final. People saying the tests are hard either didn't pay attention during lecture or didn't do the practice questions
made me not like economics even more. 0 teaching involved and the TA's write answers down with 0 explaination.

He's a “nice guy”, but being nice doesn't make you a good professor. He has an accent which makes him a bit hard to understand, is horrible at explaining concepts, doesn't use a textbook, instead makes his own lecture slides and they are horrible, uses his laptop curser to annotate instead of an iPad/tablet. inexperienced TAs do practice questions.
He is a nice guy. He always encourages us to ask questions. Plus he is quick to respond to any question asked.
He is not a good choice for this course, choose another instructor to instead of his section!

He does a flipped-classroom approach, half lecture half mini-presentations that aren't difficult if you've payed attention in the Macro/Microecon prerequisites. No text book required as he provides great notes. One of the best courses I've taken at SFU. Learned a lot of interesting theory that was relevant to the real world, with little to no math.

I'm a 4th year student, this is my first "C-" marking is very subjective, questions on exams are based on articles he gives. There are weekly group assignments worth 30% of your grade and your grade gets re distributed based on what marks your team members assign you. Makes you answer Q's in front of class, just awful, rather have the calculus.
Worst class of my undergrad. Not very clear and he doesnt answer questions very well. The TA's are not there to help (only has markers), and the class requires pure memorization of his notes.

Good news: No calculus Bad news: class is so hard you will be begging for calculus MT averages 50% or below, he is incredibly difficult to understand and his tests much much harder than his practice questions. Confrontational when you ask questions (doesn't even consider your point of view) I would rather suffer through calculus than this, its bad
use all real life cases for students to apply theories. Make sure to do the assignments and in-class practices, the exams will be a piece of cake. NO MATH
I took him about two years back. Seemed nice, though lectures can get a bit confusing due to slight structuring issues. He was new back then so I have no doubts he has been improving.

He definitely made the course much easier. I took the class with him in Summer 2024 and really enjoyed it! Just practice all the example and tutorial questions, and you'll be set to ace the course!
Make sure to listen carefully during his lectures—he gives plenty of hints for the exam questions!
Arrogant, opinionated, and defensively hostile when questioned.  I wish I had taken 207 from someone else.

Bernie is a great prof who will do everything to help you get through the course. As long as you do the practice questions and listen during lecture you'll be fine. He lets you pick the weight of your assignments on your grade. He does throw pop quizes but only a few during the semester. Fair tests and no final!

Class was boring but Bernie tried to make it as fun as possible. Basically, if you came to lecture and did the practice questions (and actually paid attention in lecture) you were guaranteed a high mark. He did catch a couple people cheating and he did end up giving them zeros (which is fair) and honestly, he gave some wisdom applicable to life.
Useless yet mandatory lectures; final based solely on book material. Avoid.

Awful prof ever! Never teaches anything, and let you have an exam based on whatever you read from book. Even through he gives the "suggested questions", it helps nothing on the exam. Awful! I would never ever get his course again!
Terrible prof. Tells stories all  class and does not teach course material. Then hard final afterwards?

Very good professor, explained concepts very well, exmas were fair, and recorded her lectures which were very beneficial. Make sure to refer to those before exams. Also do all practice questions and this course is very straightforward. Would 100% recommend.
Great prof! Pretty easy class if you are good with numbers. To do well on exams, complete all the weekly questions. The textbook is unnecessary.

Your grade is composed of 3 exams - 2 midterms and a final, but the lectures prepare you very well for these exams. All content on exams is based on questions/concepts that were reviewed in lecture. After each lecture there is an optional tutorial that helps you further understand how to solve questions. Overall, great professor, very organized.

GOAT. Test heavy, but all you have to do is keep up with the material slowly, and it's a lot of simple concepts that go more in depth math-wise from macro/micro to managerial IMO. Karen makes her lectures as interesting as possible and has lecture recordings which is one of the biggest blessings OAT.

Although your grade is fully based on exams, Karen's exams are extremely fair and very similar to the practice problems. She's a very organized professor and provides many real world examples making it easy to comprehend the material.
Very Fair prof, exams are fair, study material given is enough to do super well in exams, waitch the video recording for conceptual questions.

The best prof I have had at SFU so far. Her lectures are straight forward and provides lots of practice questions with answer keys and interesting real-world examples. Exams are very fair—often I felt like I overstudied—and reflect the practice questions and something random she'll mention in lecture. She clearly cares about her students :)

Karen recorded her lectures which I loved to use to go back on in preparation for the tests. She also provides plenty of example questions which if you master are all very similar to test questions. I enjoyed the class and thought she did a good job at presenting the material and not making it any harder than it needed to be. e
Lectures can be dry, but staying engaged pays off. Exams are key, so use Unstuck study and the textbook to prep. Grades rely heavily on the final, so come ready to perform.

Karen is a good teacher. with two midterms 25% and one final 50%, the exams were pretty straightforward and very similar to concepts we went over in class. I didn't buy the textbook although the extra practice questions would've been good. make sure to rewatch lecture recordings.

LISTEN TO THE LECTURE !! make sure to watch the lectures for the exams and midterm! she explains what to expect and as long as you do the example and tutorial questions you'll be fine. TA's were not the best. highly recommend her for BUS 207, but beware that your entire grade is based on 2 midterms and 1 final.
have fun with a final worth 75% of your grade. do not recommend.

Horrible professor. During winter break, I saw an F on my transcript. I did all the exams, got ~40% but she still failed me. Emailed her, and she was still unwilling to bump me to a D. Don't take her if you're like me. Horrible prof at Beedie.
She has a super monotone voice that makes her lectures a snoozefest. There are no assignments and you are solely graded on the 3 exams. Would not recommend

This course was already regarded as the most difficult one among all BBA courses. Considering that she was the one teaching it, one can imagine how poor the pass rate and learning experience would be for this course. The midterm average was 47%, and the TA (Yuting)'s attitude was bad; she felt she was the prof...arbitrarily set the scores.

Her lectures were interesting, especially when we linked concepts to real life! She gives weekly in-class assignments (we correct them in-class), and most grades come from 1 midterm and final. Do the practice problems and review econ theories she teaches, as they relate to test material. Otherwise tests are based on pre-recorded videos and lecture.

Her lectures are extremely boring and very disengaging. The exam is extremely hard, marked by the TA. The MT average was just 47%, and even when asking the TA about deduction marks, she just repeated my answer and not a direct answer to what I asked and finally said, "Sorry, I don't know..."

She is a poor choice to choose for BUS207. The midterm average was only 46%; the other instructors' sections are 65% and 72%. You could see how people hate this difficult class and her section! Avoid please...

Content on exams is heavily based on lectures. Lectures can be interesting at times but for the most part are mild. Exams are straightforward and questions are fair and consist of short answer and calculation questions.

She is very accessible and approachable so if you do not understand a concept, just ask her to explain in during break or after class. Importantly, the tests are directly related to the practice problems she provides, so if you can do those, you are good to go. I did not understand all the concepts at first go, it was crucial that I asked her.

Bus207 is a tough course generally but Professor Peng delivers the contents very effectively. She tests on things she actually teaches and you will certainly do well if you pay attention to her lectures. Textbook is not a must, her slides are helpful enough and cover all the content.

She has one of the most innovative teaching styles out there. She breaks down the material into 5-20 minute sections so it is easier to focus on the concept being taught. The assignments and practice questions had thorough solutions which really help one learn the content.
reply very slow and didn't care what you say.  very not helpful  don't choose her to be your teacher.

She freaking disappereard on us on the last week of class!  didnt email or post in the discussion to tell us about whats happening in regards to final, presentations, etc.  Easy course, but not so great prof.  Stick to reading the book.  Can't wait till the all written final *sigh*
